title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Wano Citizens, Unknown person #5, Orochi, Oden, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7 as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: Meanwhile, in Wano...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Lord Suki- Yaki!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Something huge was happening. Enough to call the Daimyo of each village to the flower capital.

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: The Shogun is deathly ill!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I would like open

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to continue after me as the next shown.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Of course, Lord Suki- Yaki!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But, seeing as open is currently not here,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and in case something untoward were to happen to me,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will appoint a Regent to serve until Oden's return.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I choose the man that open Doted on like a younger brother.

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: Orochi Kurozumi.

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: OROCHI?! WHY ARE YOU-

dialogue.speaker: Oden
dialogue.text: Like a younger brother?! Oden?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Humbly accept this gracious opportunity to clear the maligned Kurozumi name.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: For bestowing me with such a chance, I fear I shall remain indebted to Lord Sukiyaki and Lord Oden for as long as I live!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To stone for the foolish sins of my grandfather, I shall use what little capability I possess

dialogue.speaker: Oden
dialogue.text: to prepare the throne for Oden's Ascension!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I swear to serve as a mere puppet

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Till the day Lord Open Returns!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Please, lend me your strength!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So you're a Kurozumi, huh. Why'd you keep that a secret?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Ah, I apologize.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I Apolo- Gize...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Krakk Krakk!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Voom Voom Voom!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I see it...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I see the future!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Krashh! Voom Voom Voom Voom!

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: Will be Shogun!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Nikyokyo Kyokyo!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: UWAH!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Y- You scared me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Don't scare me like that, old lady!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Who even are you?!

dialogue.speaker: Momonosuke
dialogue.text: Do you not know your grandfather's story?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: not the specifics. I heard he committed Hara-Kiri.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No. He was killed. By the Kozuki clan!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But... surely he committed some sort of crime!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What about competing for power is a crime?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those at the top rewrite the law as they see fit.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Crimes cease to be such!

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: The previous Kozuki Shogun

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: was unable to have children. To have an heir!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The five Daimyo families that served the Kozuki grew restless.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They wondered who the next shogun would be! And as each asserted that they should be the one to take up the throne,

dialogue.speaker: Momonosuke
dialogue.text: Your grand- Father made preparations. Quietly.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 48 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
